title: Anthyllis tenuicaulis
intro: Anthyllis tenuicaulis, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial legume native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and hairy stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating conditions such as arthritis, and supporting cardiovascular health. Bioactive constituents of Anthyllis tenuicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, kidney vetch is made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements and capsules.
taxonomy: Anthyllis tenuicaulis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or bean family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf and flower structure. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Anthyllis tenuicaulis is related to other plants such as beans, peas, and legumes. The plant's classification also includes its genus, Anthyllis, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. Further classification within the plant kingdom places Anthyllis tenuicaulis in the kingdom Plantae.
